text.title: Advanced TikTok Strategies Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Advanced TikTok Strategies application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every video MUST land its hook in the first 3 seconds (pattern interrupt + claim). Intros / logos / 'hey guys' are rejected.
rule.rationale: Watch-time decays sharply after 3 seconds; the hook gate determines reach.

rule.statement: Plan MUST optimise watch-time per video, not post frequency. Posting > 2/day below 30% watch-time is rejected.
rule.rationale: Algorithm penalises low-watch-time accounts; high-frequency low-quality is worse than weekly high-quality.

rule.statement: Trend rides MUST publish within 48 hours of trend peak. Late-trend posts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Trend half-life on TikTok is 3-5 days; late publishing lands in the down-slope.

rule.statement: Plan MUST name the conversion path from video → bio → off-platform asset → goal. Untracked accounts are rejected.
rule.rationale: TikTok-only metrics don't pay rent; the off-platform path is the actual funnel.

rule.statement: ≥1 growth loop (reply-as-video / duet / stitch invitation) MUST be wired into the format. Static one-way posts are rejected as the only format.
rule.rationale: Growth loops compound reach; static posts depend on cold algorithm reach only.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
